snippets.title: First property test
snippets.code:
# pip install hypothesis
from hypothesis import given, strategies as st
from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text


@given(st.text())
def test_chunker_is_lossless(text):
    chunks = chunk_text(text, max_tokens=20)
    assert "".join(chunks) == text
snippets.explanation: One test runs against ~100 generated strings by default — empty strings, whitespace, weird Unicode, all auto-explored.

snippets.title: Combining strategies
snippets.explanation: Hypothesis explores the (text, max_tokens) joint space, finding combos that break the size invariant.

snippets.title: Adding explicit examples + shrink output
snippets.explanation: Pinned `@example`s ensure famous-bug inputs are always exercised; the shrunk falsifying example gives you the minimum reproducer.

snippets.title: Stateful (model-based) tests
snippets.explanation: Hypothesis generates random *sequences* of rule calls and checks invariants after each. Finds bugs that surface only under specific orderings of operations.

snippets.title: Tuning runtime & determinism
snippets.explanation: `derandomize=True` is great in CI for reproducible results; on a developer machine, the default random mode finds more variety over time.
